steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content:
Sentence 1: Fortunian rock from 538 million years ago can directly abut Rhaetian rock from 208.5 million years ago, with millions of years of material missing.

Sentence 2: ______ time did not stand still; the missing material was removed by erosion and weathering.

The second sentence explains why material is missing — it's not contradicting sentence 1, it's supplying the natural, obvious explanation. The transition must signal "naturally / as expected."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'On the contrary,': signals that sentence 2 contradicts sentence 1. But sentence 2 does not contradict — it explains. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Of course,': signals that what follows is the obvious, expected explanation. The erosion explanation is indeed the natural account for missing sediment — of course time kept passing and erosion occurred. ✓
  • Choice C — 'As a result,': signals that sentence 2 is a consequence of sentence 1. But the erosion happened before the gap was observed — the missing material is the result of erosion, not the other way around. Causality is reversed. ✗
  • Choice D — 'In particular,': signals that sentence 2 is a specific example of sentence 1. Sentence 2 is not an example of rocks abutting each other — it's an explanation of why material is missing. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
